'''Mitch Stringer''' (born 1 November 1983), is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}}

==Playing career==
He was brought up in [[Barnsley]], and started his youth playing career at [[Sheffield Eagles]] after impressing with local amateur team Hoyland Vikings.

Stringer signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in 2005 from [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]. In 2006 he was re-signed by the Eagles for their successful bid for promotion from National League Two.
Stringer has also played as a professional for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] where he became captain. 
 
==International==
He also made eleven appearances for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], scoring on 3 occasions. His first international game came against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], where he began the match on the bench.
He represented Scotland at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].
